feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Get to Know Display Product Availability
entry.content:
SAP has released a new Fiori app , Display Product Availability, to help you obtain product availability information relevant to Available-to-Promise.

You can use the Display Product Availability app instead of the GUI-based Fiori app, Monitor Product Availability, and take advantage of this new app’s wide-ranging capabilities.

The Display Product Availability app checks availability based on the Product Availability Check function, which provides information about the date and quantity for which a requirement (for example, a sales order item) can be fulfilled.

You can check the available quantities of your products by plant or storage location. On top of that, you can also filter this information according to different availability contexts (that is, either sales or delivery), as well as special stock types (for example, sales document, customer consignment, or no special stock).

The available quantities of your products are displayed in their corresponding base units and/or your preferred alternative units.

 

What is the difference between Display Product Availability and Monitor Product Availability?

Compared with Monitor Product Availability, the Display Product Availability app enables you to display the availability:

  • Of one or multiple products.
  • Across multiple plants, MRP areas, and their corresponding storage locations.
  • For any date starting from current date.
  • At either sales-related, or delivery-related availability contexts.
  • For different stock types, such as sales document, customer consignment, or no special stock.
  • In the corresponding base unit of the product and, if set, in an alternative unit of measure
  • With the associated CO2 emissions footprint of the products.

For example, in Monitor Product Availability you would need to enter manually the (Material) Product [TG11], the plant [1310], and the corresponding checking rule (Sales Context and No-Special Stock) [A] for the availability calculation:

The results show the availability based on the specific plant selected. Note that the possibility of checking the availability at specific dates is limited:

Comparing the same search with the results in the Display Product Availability app, you will be able to calculate the availability of the same product [TG11], by just specifying the availability context [Sales] and the special stock [No Special Stock] related to the same checking rule used previously.

You can check the product’s quantity in all the plants it is associated with, as well as other information, such as the CO2 footprint. In addition, you can calculate different availabilities for different dates by using the “Available Date” filter.

Another advantage is that you can see the availability at each storage location level, including the storage locations that does not contain any stock of the product.

 

What’s next?

Future enhancements of the Display Product Availability app include:

  • Support for other availability contexts such as production orders, service orders, and goods issue.
  • Analysis of how the available quantity is calculated given certain MRP elements, and the ATP scope of check configuration.

We appreciate your attention and hope this blog helps you in tracking product availability in Available-to-Promise!



Additional links


SAP S/4HANA advanced ATP - https://www.sap.com/products/aatp.html

Product Assistance: SAP S/4HANA Cloud - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/e7086c5bd8544fbe80a79803f459c213.html?locale=en-US&state=TEST&version=2502.VAL

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Flexible Business Role Design with App Authorization Variants
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, business catalogs are a well-established concept to pre-bundle authorizations. They combine authorizations for business functionality (selection of apps) into semantically meaningful building blocks as best practices delivered by SAP that represent tasks or sub-processes for a certain workplace within an overall business process.

While the general concept of business catalogs is being kept, I’m very happy to announce, that with the 2502 release and the introduction of IAM apps of the app authorization variant type (app authorization variants), business catalogs change from indivisible entities into entities that bundle app authorization variants that can be activated or deactivated. Consequently, app authorization variants become the smallest entity for business role design, providing greater flexibility to manage authorizations for business users including which apps they can use.

Screenshots:

Preview: deactivate individual IAM Apps in app "Maintain Business Roles"

Preview: On top: app “Maintain Business Roles”, tab "IAM Apps" is showing active/inactive app authorization variants. Below on the left: the new app “Display IAM Apps” is showing details like contained restriction types and dependencies. On the right: navigating to the app-related documentation.

Please note that this feature is only available for business catalogs that support IAM apps.

Want to explore the new feature more deeply?

What's New 2502: IAM App - App Authorization Variant

Register for the 2502 Early Release Webinar “Cross Technology Topics” on Jan 22, 2025.

entry.title: Custom CDS Views – What’s New up to 2502
entry.content:
I haven't posted any updates on the Custom CDS Views app for a while now. It's not that the app does not evolve any more but the relation between developments under the hood versus features that are visible to you as a customer changes. Also the team is focussing on additional topics in the evolving area of developer extensibility.

So here is finally another update on the Custom CDS Views app including the recent features that will be shipped with 2502 as well as a selection of what has happened behind the scenes since 2308:

Manage & Convert Legacy CDS Views

As the old Custom CDS Views app is not available any more to manage the legacy views, we introduced additional options to cover the missing functionality in the new app. You can now not only migrate views to the new app, but also delete legacy views or unfinished drafts without the need to migrate them first (2308).Delete Legacy Views

The migration functionality was also enhanced to now migrate almost every view created with the old app to the new app (2502). If you now trigger the migration, the view structure will be transferred to the new app, afterwards, the view will be analysed and then re-published (2402). The analysis checks if the legacy view can automatically be adapted to the latest modelling rules. Re-publish will then update the view and modernise the structure (e.g. update to view entity, update the access protection or resolve rework tasks if possible).
Migrate Legacy ViewsIf the analysis finds issues that cannot be fixed automatically, the view will need to be adapted to the current rules manually. You will be supported by messages with instructions in the publishing log to change your view.

You may remember that we introduced scenarios with the new Custom CDS Views app. During the migration, the scenario is derived from various properties and from the usage of the CDS view. The only reason that may still prevent a migration is, if we cannot reliably derive the scenario. On the other hand this means that there is no usage of the view, otherwise we could have derived the scenario from the usage. In this case, you may want to consider to delete the unused view or to open a ticket (BC-SRV-APS-GKE) to get migration support.

Modernised Language Features

In 2023, Simple Types were introduced as a CDS language feature to include the possibility to define types natively in CDS. These data types can be used as well in ABAP as also in ABAP CDS. For more details, refer to the Simple Types blog post from Andrea.

As also SAP begins to use this language feature in their VDM data model, we started supporting simple types with 2408. This is specifically relevant when they are used in parameters.
This does not only help supporting more modern VDM content, but when you do use simple types in your developer extensibility CDS views (and C1 release them), you will profit from the option to use it in a Custom CDS View as well.

Rework Tasks

Rework tasks help you to keep track of maintenance tasks for your Custom CDS Views. Since the beginning of Key User Extensibility, we introduced different types of rework tasks as well in the old but also for the new Custom CDS Views app. With the old app becoming obsolete, we took the chance to modernise and consolidate our rework tasks.Rework Tasks

With 2308, we moved the rework calculation away from a health check to a background job. This makes the calculation more robust & reliable. With this move, we also introduced the rework tasks in the productive system that you can see the need for maintenance specifically for your productive views as well. In the production system, the rework tasks are calculated once a week over the week-end. A calculation directly after each import is planned as well.

As we now resolve some of the rework tasks that were specific to the old app during the migration, we could get rid of these rework tasks and replaced them with one task that just reminds you on the migration of legacy CDS views as such (2402).

The rework tasks around the new access protection were also optimised. These tasks do publish a complete view stack when it is necessary. In case of a large network of views, this sometimes leads to the need to transport a large amount of views even though only one view was changed. As of 2308 this publishing process was streamlined and improved. It is now ensured that you only need to transport the views that needed to be changed (specific to your development) which also improved the performance and reliability of the process.

Deprecated and Decommissioned Elements

The Virtual Data Model deprecated elements and replaced them with new ones for quite a while now. Still, they were not removed so far. Now VDM is going one step further and started decommissioning elements. The Custom CDS Views app reacts a bit differently to decommissioned elements now than it reacts to deprecated ones. In a nutshell, we do not allow including decommissioned elements to Custom CDS Views and whenever you transport a changed Custom CDS view, you have to remove the decommissioned elements before.Decommissioned Elements

To improve dealing with deprecated & decommissioned elements, we did the following changes in 2402:

  • The element value help shows an according Icon & Tooltip (also of associations)
  • We removed decommissioned elements from the elements value help
  • We raise a warning before deleting decommissioned elements (as you can't bring them back)
  • Decommissioned elements are marked in the elements tab

Usability Improvements

Whereas the raw data preview acts as a basic, built-in possibility to check the pure structure of the data, the preview via the separate Data Browser app was introduced as a more feature-rich alternative. Until 2408, it was only used to display the current Custom CDS View. Starting with 2402, it was integrated in the data sources tab as a possibility to preview other data sources (e.g. supported VDM views) as well.Preview Options

The selection of data sources was improved in 2308 already by adding the option to display and also to search and filter by components & packages. This allows you to identify and find related data sources more easily.Data Source Selection

What's next?

The custom CDS Views app is now quite mature. Language features like explicit joins or unions would unduly increase the complexity of the key user tool and therefore remain reserved for the developer extensibility.

We will therefore in future mainly invest in better usability of the app as well as in updating and modernising existing language features.

Apart from the key user world, you will hear from me about new tools for the developer extensibility - especially for Embedded Analytics. Stay tuned 🙂

entry.title: Unlock the expertise of top SAP professionals through live sessions - Register now!
entry.content:
Don't miss the opportunity and join our live sessions


SAP Business Technology Platform

  • A guide to SAP BTP for complete beginners
    (Jan 14, 2025, 16:00 - 16:45 GMT+1)
  • Getting Started With Development Using SAP BTP ABAP Environment
    (Jan 17, 2025, 16:00 - 17:00 GMT+1)
  • Introduction to SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad
    (Jan 13, 2025, 09:00 - 11:00 GMT+1)
  • Building AI models on SAP BTP: an introduction
    (Feb 18, 2025, 08:00 - 09:30 GMT+1)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Maintaining Master Data in group reporting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    (Jan 15, 2025, 10:00 - 11:30 GMT+1)
  • Maintaining Master Data in group reporting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    (Jan 15, 2025, 10:00 - 11:30 GMT+1)
  • How to Manage User Identity in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    (Jan 16, 2025, 16:30 - 17:30 GMT+1)

As an SAP Learning Hub subscriber, you can join this live session to explore the various methods of data integration, with a focus on the best options for different scenarios. You will learn how to implement various data integration environments, ranging from a simple file import to an enterprise level data load. If you are involved in an incentive management implementation of any size, you don’t want to miss this session. 
Read more

Please note
You can find the latest live sessions here to engage and learn with SAP experts and peers.

entry.title: Enable OData Streams in RAP
entry.content:
Problem statement:
How to enable OData Streams such as excel, pdf, Images and other large objects using standard RAP Based application development without using UI5 tooling?

Solution:

With the latest SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2208 release the RAP framework now supports OData streams. It is now possible to enable your RAP application to maintain and handle Large Objects(LOBs).This feature provides end users an option to upload external files of different file formats such as PDF, XLSX , binary file format and other types hence allowing media handling.

I am on of the presenter for this topic at ABAP Conference and int the below youtube recording. This topic covers content related to how to enable large objects using RAP (ABAP RESTful Programming Model ).  Upload functionality using custom actions, download functionality using custom actions,  how to use XCO library to read and write XLSX content and some useful links which can be explored about OData Streams in RAP.


ABAP Conference recording: Enable OData Streams in RAP

Slides: https://abapconf.org/abapconf2024/#/agenda

Check for topic "Enable OData Streams in RAP" where you can find the link to download slides in pdf document.

Use cases covered:

  1. Working with Large Objects
  2. Upload Functionality using Custom Action
  3. Download Functionality using Custom Action
  4. Use XCO library to read and write XLSX Content

Code based GitHub URL's:

1. Upload and Download Functionality application : https://github.com/rvrrpk/OdataStreamsRAP

2. Download History Application : https://github.com/rvrrpk/DownloadHistory

entry.title: #10 - Review Cutover Plan - And The Importance for a Successful Go-Live
entry.content:
A successful project Go-Live is a significant milestone for any organization, marking the transition from legacy systems to a new, integrated SAP environment. The cutover phase is the final step before the system is fully operational, and the process requires meticulous planning, preparation, and execution. One of the most critical components in achieving a smooth transition is having a well-organized and comprehensive Cutover Plan.

Image 1: Cutover Overview within SAP Activate Phases

A cutover plan is a detailed, step-by-step strategy designed to ensure a smooth transition from a legacy system to a new system. It outlines all the activities and tasks that need to be completed during the cutover phase—the period right before and after the system goes live. This plan is essential to minimize disruptions, ensure data accuracy, and enable users to adopt the new system effectively.

Key Components of a Cutover Plan:

  1. Pre-Cutover Activities:
    • Data migration and validation to ensure data is accurately transferred to the new system.
    • Final system checks and testing to verify that the system is ready for Go-Live.
    • Configuration verification and adjustment based on any final requirements or changes.
  2. Cutover Execution:
    • The specific actions to take place during the transition, such as shutting down legacy systems, migrating data, and activating the new system.
    • Timing of each activity to minimize downtime and business disruption.
  3. Post-Cutover Activities:
    • Monitoring system performance and ensuring stability post Go-Live.
    • Addressing any immediate issues, such as bug fixes or system optimizations.
    • Providing training and support for end-users to ensure they are comfortable with the new system.
  4. Roles and Responsibilities:
    • Clearly defines who is responsible for each task or activity during the cutover process to ensure accountability and smooth execution.
  5. Contingency Planning:
    • Developing backup plans to handle unexpected issues or failures during the cutover process, ensuring that the business can recover quickly.

Importance of a Cutover Plan:

  • Minimizes Downtime: A well-planned cutover ensures that there is minimal disruption to business operations.
  • Ensures Data Integrity: By thoroughly checking data migration and system configurations, the plan ensures that business-critical data remains accurate.
  • Facilitates Smooth System Transition: Clear instructions on roles, tasks, and timing ensure all team members are aligned and that the Go-Live happens as planned.
  • Enhances User Adoption: Post cutover training and support help users get up to speed quickly with the new system.

 

Image 2: Ingredients for a Successful Cut-Over

 

You want to check the available Cutover Template under SAP Activate Roadmap.

In short, a cutover plan is a critical tool for managing the final stages of an implementation project, ensuring a smooth transition and successful adoption of the new system.

Tip: Don't feel overwhelmed, preparation is key, and you'll have an implementation team to guide you in planning and defining the cutover strategy. By understanding what to expect in advance, you'll find it easier to follow the discussions and collaborate effectively with the team throughout the process.

Stay curious and never stop learning!

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Ana Barreira serves as the Global Onboarding Advisor for the SAP Transportation Management (TM) solution within the Supply Chain Management area. She provides expert guidance and support to new SAP customers from contract signature through to successful go-live. With eleven years of experience—three of which have been as a Global Onboarding Advisor, and eight years as an SAP Consultant—Ana specializes in the SAP TM solution. She has successfully supported full-cycle customer implementation projects, assisted partners, and contributed to internal enablement efforts

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Ana_Barreira  or @Linh-Hoang .

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Unified access to documentation about Identity and Access Management on SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
Benefit from the one-stop shop for identity and access management (IAM) documentation on SAP Help Portal.
Get an overview of apps for user and identity management, roles and authorization, and authentication.
Additionally, let the documentation guide you through the tasks related to IAM for the different implementation phases (prepare, realize, run, and upgrade) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and integrated products. This includes documentation on initial system access and on tasks that need to be completed before, during, and after the upgrade of SAP S/4HANA Cloud solutions.

Preview: one-stop shop for identity and access management (IAM) documentation

Interested? 

Identity and access management (IAM) documentation on SAP Help Portal

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2409 FD3
entry.content:
With the latest release for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview of these features.

Simplified User Experience for Milestones

In our ongoing efforts to streamline the user experience, we've merged the Scoping is Completed milestone with the Complete Activity button in the Define Scope app. This means that once you've defined your scope, you can simply choose Complete Activity to confirm it. This action will, just as before, initiate the loading of your scope into the workspace, as well as the calculation of relevant configuration activities. That’s why the Assign Deployment Target activity has become the first one in the Activities list on the Overview page in order to improve the workflow.

Additionally, we've updated some of the activity descriptions and naming for clarity. For example, the milestone previously known as Confirm Scope and Organizational Structure Phase is Completed is now referred to as the Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure activity. What’s more, you can initiate the deployment of your scope and organizational structure also from the Deployment Target app. These changes aim to make the process more intuitive and understandable.

Screenshot of the scope confirmation in the Define Scope app

Enhanced Terminology

To better reflect the nature of your activities and improve overall terminology, we've replaced the term “project” with “workspace” throughout SAP Central Business Configuration. This update aligns with the retirement of the “project” concept since SAP Central Business Configuration mainly serves as a tool for configuration, and not for project management.

Screenshot of the updated terminology in the Settings app

Unified Themes for the In-App Help

To provide a cohesive and seamless user experience, we've aligned the theme of the in-app help with the theme you select in your personal user settings. This ensures that your experience remains consistent and visually appealing, reducing any potential disconnect caused by differing themes.

Screenshot of the light theme of the in-app help

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2409 FD3. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Embedded Analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Leveraging Data for Success
entry.content:
Data plays a pivotal role in shaping strategies and driving business success. The latest episode of the Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud podcast, hosted by the SAP Product Expert Katharina Klug, delves into the field of embedded analytics within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This episode features Daniel Spokoinyi and Sander van Kolck, esteemed analytics experts from SAP partner CTAC, who bring their wealth of experience to discuss how businesses can leverage these tools for optimal performance.

Click here and listen to the Episode 115: Embedded Analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Leveraging Data for Success

 

Understanding the Need for Embedded Analytics

Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud represents a significant shift in how businesses interact with their data. Traditionally, analytics were somewhat isolated from the main ERP systems, but SAP S/4HANA Cloud integrates analytics directly within its framework. This integration allows businesses to gain actionable insights much more efficiently, driving what is often referred to as "insight to action".

Katharina highlights a crucial point: many businesses transitioning to S/4HANA Cloud initially overlook analytics, leading to challenges in realizing the full potential of their data. Our guests emphasize the importance of including analytics in the ERP discovery phase to align with business needs and strategy effectively. This early inclusion can address common roadblocks such as unawareness, fear of complexity, and overly ambitious implementation plans.

Check out the Link Collection page for Embedded Analytics in the SAP Community

 

The Journey with Embedded Analytics

Sander shares valuable insights into overcoming these challenges by getting to know the customer's unique reporting landscape. No two businesses are the same, and analytics solutions need to be tailored accordingly. This involves understanding the critical reports and the core questions they need to address, ensuring a smooth transition and continuity in the business processes.

Daniel discusses the mindset shift required when embedding analytics directly within the system. Rather than viewing analytics as an optional add-on, businesses should see it as an integral part of their ERP journey, starting from pre-sales and extending through implementation.

 

Overcoming Challenges for Successful Analytics Integration

The podcast episode stresses the importance of collaboration between analytics consultants and functional consultants. Such collaboration ensures that while technical aspects are handled efficiently, the end-user experience remains a priority, leading to a more purposeful and results-driven approach to analytics.

Additionally, Sander and Daniel underline the need for SAP to offer business content and more transparent pathways for transitioning existing reports to the S/4HANA Cloud environment. This involves creating more standard SAP Analytics Cloud stories and providing comprehensive tools that guide customers in selecting the most suitable reports for their needs.

 

The Road Ahead with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

This episode is not just a discussion but a call to action for businesses to embrace the potential of embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The integration of analytics into every functional stream is crucial, providing valuable insights that drive real business actions, such as maintaining data quality in business partner records or optimizing supply chain processes.

Katharina, alongside her guests, points out that SAP is actively rolling out innovative analytics solutions, such as the Group Financial Statement Review Booklet, leveraging generative AI for ready-to-use business insights. This commitment to enhancing business content demonstrates SAP's dedication to delivering valuable tools that empower businesses to make smarter decisions.

 

Listen to the Full Episode 115: Embedded Analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Leveraging Data for Success

For those interested in diving deeper into the transformative impact of embedded analytics, don't miss out on this enlightening podcast episode. Katharina Klug and her expert guests, Daniel and Sander, provide a wealth of knowledge and practical advice for leveraging data to achieve business success. Tune in to discover how embedded analytics can support your business processes and decision-making capabilities.

Click here and tune in now!

 

Do you want to contact us or send your suggestion for the next episodes? Send us an e-mail to insides4@sap.com

 

Podcast Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud product insights, implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Check all the episodes at sap.com/podcasts, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, TuneIn, and more audio platforms.

entry.title: New Features for Key User Extensibility Transport Management in S/4HANA Cloud 3SL 2502
entry.content:
Introduction 

Did you ever need to manage the (parallel) implementation and maintenance of key user extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, 3-system landscape? 

If the answer is ‘Yes’ you might have recognized that the Export Software Collection and Import Collection apps have some restrictions, which make it difficult to separate corrections from new development in the transport management process. 

In his blog post Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3SL (2): Parallel Implementation and Maintenance Thomas Schneider described the basic principles of parallel implementation and maintenance. In the following I will dive deeper into the process and highlight how new features in release 2502 help to overcome the restrictions for key user extensibility.

 

Working with Software Collections in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, 3-System Landscape

With the introduction of the 3-system landscape, the process of transporting software collections for key user extensibility has changed significantly due to the introduction of the test system. But let’s look back at the 2-system landscape first, where key user extensions are created and tested in the quality system. After successful testing, they are exported with the next software collection version. After the export, the software collection version is ready for import into the production system.  

Figure 1: Transport Process in 2-System Landscape 

The example in Figure 1 shows two versions of the same software collection that were exported from the quality system after the release decision. They were imported to the production system afterwards. The ‘Changed (and Deleted) Items’ option is used first to export the newly developed Item 1 and Item 2. After that the ‘Hotfix’ option was used for the correction Item 1’ without exporting the changed Item 2’. This process is sufficient to manage parallel corrections and development in the 2-system landscape. 

Let’s look at the 3-system landscape now. In that landscape, key user extensions are created and tested in the development system. After that, they are exported to the test system for integration testing. The release decision on whether a version is ready for production is now made in the test system using the Import Collection app. However, the same rules for exporting and importing software collections are applied:  

  1. Dependencies are checked during the export of software collections. During the export, the Export Software collection app will always check the dependencies between key user items. With that all changes that are needed in production are contained in the same version or have been exported beforehand. 
  2. A higher version of the same software collection can’t overtake a lower version. The Import Collection app will make sure that the import happens in the same sequence as the export.  

The combination of both helps to reduce the risk of issues in production to a minimum. However, when doing parallel development and maintenance this leads to restrictions that are displayed in Figure 2 and Figure 3.  

Figure 2: Hotfix must include unwanted item 

Figure 2 shows an example where the hotfix was forced to include new development on Item 2’ because the corrected Item 1’ declares a dependency on it. Hence, new development Item 2’ would be imported to production with the hotfix. 

Figure 3: Rejected release decision blocks correction 

As shown in Figure 3, hotfixes can’t bypass lower versions containing new development. The release decision of the second ‘Changed (and Deleted) Items’ export has been declined. Hence, it blocks the immediate delivery of the subsequent hotfix.  

Both restrictions have been addressed by new features in release 2502 which will be described below starting with the dependency checks for the hotfix export. 

 

Dependency Checks for Hotfix Export 

Due to the dependency checks performed by the Export Software Collection app hotfixes often need to include content that isn’t part of the actual correction. 

Until to the 2408 release, the app checked which key user extensibility items are used by items contained in the hotfix. If the used items had the status ‘New’, ‘Changed’ or ‘Deleted’ they needed to be added to the hotfix. Otherwise, the export checks failed. 

In a 3-system landscape however, this led to an unwanted side effect when performing corrections and new development in parallel. If the item to be corrected used other items that were changed by new development, the app enforced the inclusion of these items into the hotfix. However, the new development might not have been ready for production, yet. 

With 2502 the checks for exporting software collection hotfixes have been reduced significantly. The dependency check for the hotfix export option has been changed in the 3-system landscape.  

Figure 4: Hotfix with relaxed dependency check 

As you can see in Figure 4 it’s no longer mandatory to include the used item 2’. The hotfix leaves Item 2 unchanged in test and production system. 

With that, it can be decided whether the change to a used item shall be contained in the hotfix or be exported in a later version. The Export Software collection app still helps to identify such dependencies. The check hasn’t been fully removed, but its severity has been reduced to a warning for items with the status ‘Changed’ or ‘Deleted’. However, it is still mandatory to include items with status ‘New’. Since new items have never been exported before, using such an item would cause import issues. Hence, they must still be included.  

 

Discarding Extensibility Collection Versions 

After exporting a hotfix and importing it to the test system, users encounter the next restriction in the 3-system landscape. The hotfix can’t be forwarded and imported to production until the same is done for all previous versions of the same software collection. 

This rule is part of the safety net for the key user extensibility transport process and prevents the production system from becoming inconsistent due to incorrect transport sequence. Until now, there was no possibility to ‘clear the way’ for corrections to the production system for key user extensibility.  

With 2502 key user extensibility collection versions can be discarded in the Import Collection app. 

Figure 5: Discarding software collections in test system 

As you can see in Figure 5 the ‘Discard’ action has been used after the declined release decision. The information about the discarded version was sent to the production system. After that it was possible to forward the hotfix and import it. 

The ‘Discard’ action has been available for other collection types (e.g., developer extensibility) already. The action changes the status of the collection version to ‘Discarded’ and prevents further actions. The result depends on the previous state of the collection version: 

  • Ready for Import: The collection version can’t be imported into the current system, nor can it be forwarded for import into a subsequent system. A discarded collection version is automatically transported to all subsequent systems (with status ‘Discarded’) so that any dependencies on the current version no longer exist. 
  • Imported (in test system only): The collection version can’t be imported into a subsequent system. A discarded collection version is automatically transported to all subsequent systems so that any dependencies on the current version no longer exist. 

With that, software collection versions that block delivery of a correction to the production system can be ‘moved out the way’. However, this also means that the discarded collection versions can’t be forwarded to the production system later. The extensibility items on these versions need to be exported in a higher version of the same software collection to be consolidated to production later.  

Figure 6: Consolidating a discarded item 

In Figure 6 a software collection version was discarded to enable the hotfix for Item 1’’. However, the changed Item 2’ was not forwarded to production due to that. Another hotfix for the, now unchanged, Item 2’ was needed to consolidate it to the production system. 

The consolidation of discarded items can either be done with another hotfix for or during continued development, e.g. with another changed Item 2’’. However, there is the risk of missing some of the items that have been discarded. To counter that, the safety net for key user extensibility has been enhanced.  

 Figure 7: Consolidating an unchanged item 

As can be seen in Figure 7, Item 2 has never been consolidated to production system. During the next export of ‘Changed and Deleted Items’ Item 2 was included automatically.  

The Export Software Collection app checks the status of exported items in the production system. If a certain version of an item hasn’t reached the production system yet, the item is included in the next export when using the option ‘Changed and Deleted Items’, even when the items status is ‘Unchanged’. 

 

Conclusion

To manage corrections and new development occurring in parallel, the export option ‘Changed and Deleted Items’ should be used for new development, while corrections should be delivered via hotfixes. The relaxed export checks for hotfixes make it easier to separate corrections from new development, keeping the hotfix small. This ensures it can be imported to production without any side effects. 

The option to discard allows previous versions of the same software collection to be moved out of the way. However, the discarded collections can’t be imported to production later. Another version of the software collection will need to be exported to consolidate the discarded items, eventually. To ensure discarded items aren’t forgotten, the next export with ‘Changed and Deleted Items’ will automatically include those items, even if they are ‘Unchanged’. 

With these new features it becomes easier to manage parallel correction and development within a single software collection in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-system landscape beginning with release 2502. Please keep in mind that these features don’t apply to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2-system landscape. 

Additional Information and Links 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Three-System Landscape | SAP Community 

Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public E... - SAP Community 

Transport Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3SL (2):... - SAP Community

entry.title: GROW with SAP, Resource 1 Pager (Base & Premium)
entry.content:
This is a short blog post - and that's the point.

In my previous blogs, we delved into the components of the GROW with SAP, base edition, and the GROW with SAP, premium edition bundles  - but these are long reads.

For your convenience, the adoption office has created the resource pictured and linked in the image below with key information and resources for each entitlement.

Link to document

Click the image to download

Note that some resources, coloured in grey, are not yet available - the document will be updated if/when they are made so.

If you have any additional/alternate resources to recommend, please suggest them in the comments section!

entry.title: Key Facts in SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
entry.content:
Overview High Level

With this blog, we aim to guide you through the MBC Onboarding Process, Roles and Responsibilities, and SAP Links to relevant information about this topic.

SAP offers a revolutionary way to handle banking transactions with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity based on integration services deployed in the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This service provides a Financial Services Network that directly connects the customer SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system with the Financial Services Institutions (FSIs), providing a secure and efficient digital channel. It also offers embedded SWIFT connectivity.

One of the key benefits of this solution is that it automates all the manual and error-prone steps associated with the execution and reconciliation of payments, order-to-cash applications, and order-entry documents. The payment status and cash positions are automatically updated in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system once updates are available from the banks. As a result, it provides measurable improvement to the accounts payable and accounts receivable functions.

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is an additional license that provides a managed onboarding service that helps you complete all the necessary tasks to configure the data exchange and establish a connection between your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system and your Financial Services Institutions.

 

Landscape

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity exposes two system landscapes: test and production.

 

Onboarding process – Phases details

During the Onboarding process to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity, two connections will be set up:

  1.            From the ERP system to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity.
  2.            From SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity to the banks or Financial Services Institutions.

 

MBC Phased Onboarding

The MBC Setup is based on a phased onboarding approach:

 

  •  Phase 1 – Scoping:

Purpose: This phase determines the optimal setup best suited for your use case.

Roles involved: Customer and MBC Scoping Team

Activities: The MBC Scoping team collaborates with the customer to ensure a shared understanding of the objectives, constraints, and requirements. The customer receives the “Scoping Questionnaire,” which contains the required information to collect from the FSIs.

 

  •  Phase 2 – Preboarding:

Purpose: Prepare all necessary certificates, confirm SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition configuration readiness, and obtain technical connectivity details from your FSIs.

Roles involved: Customer and MBC Onboarding Team.   

Activities: The customer receives the Preboarding documentation (Welcome Pack, To-do Checklist, and Bank Connection (if applicable)). The customer collects the information requested in the preboarding documentation from the FSIs and SWIFT (if applicable). Once the preboarding documentation is completed, please email the MBC Onboarding Team under sapmbconboarding@sap.com.

 

  •   Phase 3 – Technical:

Purpose: The SAP MBC Onboarding Team configures your test tenant connectivity between your FISs and the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

Roles involved: Customer and the MBC Onboarding Project Team (CSM - Customer Success Manager and TIE – Technical Integration Engineer)

Activities: The Scoping and Preboarding phase must be closed. The MBC Onboarding Project Team consists of one CSM and one TIE, which will be assigned directly to the customer. The SAP MBC Onboarding Team will set up the MBC connections with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system and the FSIs, and SWIFT (if applicable). Tests are executed.

 

  •   Phase 4 – Validation:

Purpose: Validation of the end-to-end process.

Roles involved: Customer and the MBC Onboarding Project Team (CSM - Customer Success Manager and TIE – Technical Integration Engineer)

Activities: The validation of the end-to-end process in the test systems is a critical step in ensuring the success of our onboarding process. Upon successful sign-off, you will seamlessly transition to the Promotion to Production phase.

 

  •   Phase 5 – Promotion to Production:

Purpose: Penny test and Set up the Production system.

Roles involved: Customer and the MBC Onboarding Project Team (CSM - Customer Success Manager and TIE – Technical Integration Engineer)

Activities: The production system is fully set up, and the penny test has been successfully executed to verify the correct processing of upcoming payments. Once everything is confirmed, the onboarding process will be concluded, and you will be promptly handed over to the SAP global support team.

 

Roles and responsibilities

  1.            Tenant Provisioning Team (SAP):
  • Responsible for provisioning and decommissioning tenants.
  • Ensures the allocation of test and productive landscapes to the customer.
  • Manages the entire process of provisioning resources, known as tenants, for the customer.

             

  1.            MBC Security Team:
  • Notifies the customer's designated tenant administrator once the tenants are provisioned.
  • Oversees the security aspects related to the provisioned tenants.
  • Collaborates with the customer to ensure security protocols and standards are met.

 

  1.            Customer Tenant Administrator:
  • Acts as the primary point of contact for the allocated tenants.
  • Manages user access and permissions within the assigned tenants.
  • Collaborates with the MBC Security Team to enforce security policies and procedures.
  • Facilitates communication between the customer's organization and the MBC service provider regarding tenant-related issues.
  • Ensures compliance with contractual agreements and security protocols.

 

  1.            Onboarding Team (Tenant Administrators):
  • During the onboarding process, responsible for managing tenant access to MBC Security.
  • Collaborates with the MBC Security Team to configure initial access settings and permissions.
  • Provides necessary training and guidance to users within the organization on how to utilize MBC resources securely.
  • Assists in troubleshooting access-related issues during the onboarding phase.
  • These responsibilities are distributed as per the Operating Model, ensuring clear delineation of tasks and effective collaboration between SAP, the MBC Security Team, and the customer's designated administrators. For further details, refer to the Operating Model page.

 

Links:

  • SAP Help Portal - SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
  • SAP Help Portal – Frequently Questions
  • Blog SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) – All You Need to Know

entry.title: Precise Control of Fiori App Access with the App Authorization Variants
entry.content:
Introduction

On January 17, 2025, my colleague @christian_hochwarth officially announced the availability of App Authorization Variants in his blog SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Flexible Business Role Design with App Authorization Variants.  This new feature gives our customers a highly desirable capability to precisely control individual Fiori App access; one step up from past shortcoming of one business catalog grants access to multiple Fiori apps.

This blog is going to give an in-depth discussion on this new feature on a 2502 Release system of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

Background

Within SAP’s Identity Authorization Management (IAM), user access to transactions or Fiori apps are controlled by the Business User Roles, such as Purchase Manager. Between Business User Roles and apps, there is one layer called Business Catalogs.  You can view Business User Roles as an umbrella supported by one or more Business Catalogs. The real access control power is at these Business Catalogs as illustrated in below figure: when a user has been assigned the Business User Role with Business Catalog 1, he/she gets all access to four Apps, 1 through 4, regardless the App 2 might not be necessary for the user’s job description.

Business Roles, Business Catalogs and Fiori Apps

By taking the business persona into consideration, we give certain business catalogs access to more than one app for the convenience of business users.  For example, the Business Catalog Master Data - Business Partner Display grant access to 20 applications, can display customer data in accounting area as well as in sales area. This thinking is valid when we targeted small and medium enterprises for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition several years ago.

Business Catalog Master Data - Business Partner Display Grants Access to 20 Apps

Fast forward to today’s environment, this one-to-many (one business catalog to multiple Fiori apps) access control thinking shows its limitation, especially when we have GDPR in place (don’t expose data to an unintended audience), and more and more medium to large enterprises are embracing the Public Cloud with strict roles and responsibilities.  My customer told me clearly that she wanted a very limited number of apps to one user, all clearly defined by the job role.

This request was voted highly in our Customer Influence Council called Improved Flexibility in Business Role Design. SAP answered the call.

 

App Authorization Variants in a Nutshell

The solution is to add a layer called App Authorization Variants between the Business Catalogs and the Apps.  Each app is controlled by an App Authorization Variant (AAV).  For example, to access App 1, AAV-A is needed.  App 3 was granted access by Business Catalogs 1 and 2. Now you need either AAV-C or AAV-D to access it.

App Authorization Variants between the Business Catalog and the Apps

With this new layer of control, we can easily meet the requirements of our customers in precisely controlling who can access which app.  For example, by removing AAV-B and AAV-C, the user can only access App 1, 3 and 4 as desired. App 3 is granted by the Business Catalog 2, while App 4 is granted from both Business Catalogs.  App 2 can no longer be accessed even it is part of Business Catalog 1.

 Precise App Access Control with the App Authorization Variant

Now let me give more formal introduction of the App Authorization Variant.

The official name of AAV is “IAM apps of the app authorization variant type”.  What does that mean?

IAM Apps are relatively new to our Public Cloud. They are all IAM related for the name’s sake. We divide these apps into eleven types. The App Authorization Variant is one of them, and relevant to this discussion. In total, there are 7,851 IAM Apps as of today.  Among them, 7,709 are App Authorization Variant type. The AAV is the primary user of the IAM Apps for now, at the 2502 Release of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Different Types of IAM Apps

For the sake of convenience, I will use the term App Authorization Variant (AAV) throughout the blog.

Not all business catalogs support AAV yet.  Among 2,195 Business Catalogs, 1,760 of them supports AAV.  That is roughly 80%. It is not bad considering we have so many business catalogs.

1,760 (or 80%) Business Catalogs Support IAM Apps

 

Examples of Using App Authorization Variants

To access the AAV information, we usually start from app Maintain Business Roles. Take SAP delivered business user role template BR_ADMINISTRATOR as an example. This role contains 54 business catalogs, and 100 IAM Apps (all in App Authorization Variant type). All of these AAV’s are active. You can individually select each one of them to deactivate or activate.

IAM Apps in Business User Role Template BR_ADMINISTRATOR

Now let’s take a closer look at transaction F1303, Maintain Business Users. In the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library, it lists five business catalogs.  That means as long as you have one of these five business catalogs in your business role definition, the user can access app Maintain Business Users.

Business Catalogs Associated with App Maintain Business Users

Now I am going to apply AAV to the user management.  To showcase its effects, I use a 2408 Release system.  Because I have two accounts there, one for System Admin (GY) and another one for Testing (TY). The AAV feature was enabled on this 2408 Release system as an Early Adopter.

First, I create a new business user role by copying from SAP role template BR_ADMINISTRATOR, called it ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR. The user TY is assigned to it.  To simplify the user role, I removed all business catalogs but five IAM related ones.

Five Business Catalogs within Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR

There are 15 IAM Apps associated with this Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR.  Three of them are user management related as highlighted in green.

15 IAM Apps associated with this Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR

As the Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR is created by copying from a standard SAP template BR_ADMINISTRATOR, all five Launchpad Spaces are copied over.  When user TY logs on to the system, due to removing 49 unrelated business catalogs, four of these Launchpad Spaces are no longer there.  There is only one Administration Launchpad Space visible. There are 11 Pages belong to this Space, only the Identity and Access Management Page has active app tiles listed.  There are no app tiles shown up on other Pages. We are going to focus on three apps surrounded by the green boxes.

The Identity and Access Management Page for User TY

Now I am going to showcase the effects of AAV.

Scenario 1 – Removing App Tile Display Technical Users

Enter the Edit mode of the Business User Role ZYU_BR_ADMINISTRATOR. Select the IAM App Display Technical Users, hit the Deactivate button, the Save button.

Deactivate IAM App Display Technical Users

After refreshing the web browser, user TY only see three app tiles in the Users and Roles section.  The app tile Display Technical Users is invisible.

The Display Technical Users App Is No Longer part of Identity and Access Management Page for User TY

Scenario 2 – Removing User Role Assignment Capability

To assign a user to the business role, we can hit the Add button as below in the app Maintain Business Roles.

Visible Add button in the app Maintain Business Roles

Now I plan to remove this Add privilege from user TY, I can deactivate the IAM App Assign Business Users to Business Roles F1303_22_TRAN.

Deactivated IAM App Assign Business Users to Business Roles F1303_22_TRAN

After refreshing the web browser, user TY only see three app tiles in the Users and Roles section.  The app tile Maintain Business Roles app is still there, as intended.  By entering the app Maintain Business Roles, user TY can still add users to a business role!  Nothing has changed.

Why? Is it a bug of AAV?

The answer is "No".  If we check the IAM App list carefully, we can see that there is another IAM App Assign Business Roles to Business Users F1492_22_TRAN. This IAM App belongs to a different App F1492.  "Assigning a user to a role" and "assigning a role to a user" are the same thing but different sayings.  They are all performed by the same transaction.

Note: Now I understand better that a transaction and a Fiori app are not identical.  There are one or many transactions embedded in a Fiori app.  The same transaction can be embedded in different Apps, such as F1303 and F1492 in this case.

Deactivated IAM Apps Assign Business Users to Business Roles F1303_22_TRAN and Assign Business Roles to Business Users F1492_22_TRAN

After deactivating both IAM Apps, I can no longer edit the users in the app Maintain Business Roles.  The Add and Remove buttons are greyed out.

User TY Cannot Edit Users in Maintain Business Roles App

I am just curious, how about I try to add a role to the user from app Maintain Business User? The Add and Remove button are also greyed out.

User TY Cannot Assign Business Roles in Maintain Business User App

The AAV works as intended.  Bravo!

 

Conclusion

To meet the customer’s business needs in different environment, SAP released a new feature called App Authorization Variants to create a one-to-one access control between a transaction/app and a business user role. The App Authorization Variants become the smallest entity for business user role design.  With this feature, administrators can precisely grant an app/transaction access to a user from a business role.  There won't be any data exposure to unintended users.

 

References

  • Blog: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Flexible Business Role Design with App Authorization Variants
  • What’s New 2502: IAM App – App Authorization Variant
  • Blog: User Management in a Nutshell for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

entry.title: Watch the Replays of Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package combining solutions, services, community support, and learnings to help businesses of all sizes successfully adopt cloud ERP. At its core lies SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, our fully-fledged cloud ERP designed to optimize processes and drive intelligent, sustainable operations. 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a biannual event showcasing live sessions led by product experts. These sessions provide an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Spanning several days, the series offers expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A, giving participants a first look at exciting updates and the opportunity to get their questions answered directly.

From January 21 to January 24, 2025, we're hosting 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. Don't miss this chance and watch the recordings on demand– anywhere, anytime.

Please note that, as the series is still ongoing, not all recordings have been uploaded to the SAP Learning Hub yet. Thus, resulting in a "no classes available" note when following some of the links. 

 

Artificial Intelligence & AnalyticsWatch here
Enterprise Portfolio & Project ManagementWatch here
Professional ServicesWatch here
Solution Orders and Subscription BillingWatch here
SalesWatch here
Cross Technology TopicsWatch here
Environment, Health, and SafetyWatch here
RetailWatch here
Supply ChainWatch here
SAP User ExperienceWatch here
Public SectorWatch here
ManufacturingWatch here
Sourcing and ProcurementWatch here
Governance, Risk, and ComplianceWatch here
ServiceWatch here
FinanceWatch here
Two-Tier ERPWatch here
ExtensibilityWatch here
Product ComplianceWatch here
Asset ManagementWatch here
SAP Activate, SAP Cloud ALM, Test Automation ToolWatch here

 

An active SAP Learning Hub license is required to consume the recordings. Find out how to sign up​

  • As a customer:​ Access to at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition is required. Follow these steps to sign up:​
    1.  Navigate to the sign-up page and click on "Sign Up".
    2. Authenticate yourself with your S-user ID and password.​
    3. The system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalogue. This process may take up to 2 hours.​
    4. You will receive a registration confirmation via e-mail once the registration process is complete.​
  • As a partner: Partners can access the recordings through their SAP Partner Edge Edition.​
  • As an SAP internal: Register here for the SAP Learning Hub Internal Edition (free of charge).

Experiencing issues? Make sure to be logged on before registering. In case of any other issues, reach out to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Live Product Expert Sessions.

Explore more replays of our past Early Release Series and find out more about our live product expert sessions here.

entry.title: Settlement Documentation Revamped
entry.content:
Hi there!

Are you new to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and want to understand the settlement process in Overhead Accounting? Or perhaps you're an experienced user with specific questions? Either way, you're in the right place.

The settlement process in Overhead Accounting can be complex, but we've made it easier for you to understand by updating and restructuring the product assistance, which is available on the SAP Help Portal. Here, you can find a detailed overview of the process as well as important settings to consider.

Key Resources

- Settlement: This is your go-to resource for a detailed overview of the settlement process. It covers everything from basic concepts to advanced settings.

- Management Accounting and Margin Analysis: FAQ: Here, you'll find answers to the most commonly asked questions about Management Accounting and Margin Analysis, which are crucial to understand Overhead Accounting.

Both resources are based on the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release: 2502 (February 2025).

Why This Matters

A vast amount of information is available on different platforms and finding the right answers can be challenging. Our updated documentation aims to streamline this process by providing you with clear and concise information all in one place.

Want to get further details?

Refer to Overhead Accounting.

Do you have questions or comments?

Don’t hesitate to post them in the comments section. We’d love to hear from you and help you further.

entry.title: Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Release
entry.content:
Welcome to a new era of innovation and seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502! As we usher in 2025, it's the perfect time to celebrate the incredible journey we've shared with our public Cloud ERP community. Over the years, our customers have quickly gained extensive upgrade experience, all thanks to our continuous and seamless feature delivery. Reflecting on this progress, it's astonishing to note that our 33 major SaaS ERP release upgrades are equivalent to encapsulating 130+ years of upgrade experience based on the traditional on-premise deployment models. This showcases the high standard of our SaaS model. With the clean core approach and full lifecycle compliance embedded, we enable customers to harness the latest features instantly.

As our technological landscape continues to evolve, SAP remains at the forefront, driving innovations that empower businesses. I am excited to walk you through my key highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

Get ready for an exciting array of updates, including powerful AI enhancements, expanded capabilities of our generative AI co-pilot, Joule, and groundbreaking ERP innovations. Our 2502 release is here to further automate your operations, underpin agility in a cloud-first environment and drive more of your processes towards zero touch. Stay tuned as we dive into these transformative developments.

If you prefer watching rather than reading, you are welcome to check out my product update video: 

Figure 1: Check out my top highlights of the release in this video. 

 

AI-Powered UX Enhancements: Transforming User Interaction with Joule

Joule, our generative AI co-pilot, revolutionizes user experience by streamlining tasks through instant insights and intuitive navigation via natural language input. One notable feature is the new personalized card creation, enabling users to swiftly access vital ERP data such as purchase orders and delivery updates, thus, enhancing decision-making processes.

Figure 2: Get instant insights and swift access to your data with our natural language, generative AI copilot, Joule.

 

 

Our AI-driven UX innovations, AI-assisted smart summarization and AI-assisted easy filter, initially released as beta shipments in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, have now become generally available. Smart summarization leverages artificial intelligence to condense complex data into easily understandable summaries, saving time and boosting efficiency. Easy filter allows users to apply detailed filters using natural language, simplifying data exploration.

Figure 3: AI-assisted smart summarization saves time and boosts efficiency by condensing complex data into easily understandable summaries.

 

 

Figure 4: AI-assisted easy filter simplifies data exploration and reduces effort.

 

 

As we progress with AI-assisted UX features, the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces remarkable advancements in beta testing. These include: 

  • AI-assisted easy enterprise search,
  • AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home,  
  • and AI-assisted error explanation.  

Sign up through this link to start testing.

 

Enhancing Carbon Accountability with SAP Green Ledger

SAP Green Ledger is a transformative financial solution at the heart of carbon accounting. This is designed to integrate seamlessly with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability. By extending the scope of financial accounting to encompass carbon, it applies the principles of double-entry accounting to carbon quantities. These are posted on dimensions relevant to business steering, like profit centers and segments, offering a tangible perspective on carbon impact from provisioning through to delivery. The value proposition of SAP Green Ledger lies in its ability to track, value, and report the carbon footprint of end-to-end operations, with an emphasis on producing auditable and trustworthy reports. By analyzing carbon data alongside financial attributes, it generates insights pivotal for informed decision-making.

 

Figure 5: SAP Green Ledger integrates seamlessly with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability.

 

 

The key capabilities include integration support with other systems, replication of financial master data, importation and enrichment of material movements with carbon information and exporting carbon data for group reporting. Through its robust allocation management and comprehensive visualization, SAP Green Ledger allows businesses to compare carbon data directly with financial data, enhancing strategic understanding and operational transparency.

 

Enhancing Quality Management with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Expanding the “Manage Certificate Receipts” App with Create Mode

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces the create mode in the “Manage Certificate Receipts” app, building on the foundation of innovations introduced in the 2408 release. The original app allowed users to manage and link quality certificates to purchasing documents and goods receipts which improves compliance and traceability. The new Create Mode adds flexibility by enabling users to record certificates received before goods arrive, e.g., by email, and link them to material records upon arrival.

Figure 6: Record certificate receipts before goods arrival, ensuring smoother inventory processes.

 

This enhancement also improves traceability by providing access to existing certificates tied to purchasing documents and items, ensuring complete visibility across processes. Reduce potential error sources and strengthen both transparency and compliance processes with the further evolution of our certificate management capabilities. This enhancement solidifies the Manage Certificate Receipts App as a vital tool for higher efficiency.

 

Unlocking Transformation with GROW with SAP on AWS: A New Era in Cloud ERP

Having your Experience evolution on mind, I´d like to point you to GROW with SAP on AWS, an initiative that seamlessly integrates SAP's undeniable ERP expertise with AWS's expansive cloud infrastructure. With this, SAP is offering transformative solutions for organizations adopting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This alliance is a milestone in delivering extraordinary value, tailored for a diverse array of industries, scalability, security, and innovation. GROW with SAP on AWS features a modular design with prebuilt, industry-specific capabilities which allows businesses to scale effortlessly. Rapid deployment is facilitated by proven methodologies such as SAP Activate. Businesses can transition to even swifter deployments.

Figure 7: GROW with SAP on AWS.

 

 

The collaboration on AWS Marketplace simplifies procurement and accelerates adoption, empowering companies to thrive with agility and resilience in today's digital economy. GROW with SAP on AWS emerges beyond ERP, as a transformative enabler that drives the digital transformation frontier forward.

 

Expanding Opportunities with Scalable Solutions: Empowering Partners Through ABAP Cloud Add-Ons

With the general availability of scalable solutions on stack, partners are now equipped to build and extend solutions for their customers, creating significant value and expanding capabilities. This empowerment enables partners to develop, package, and broadly distribute their solutions as ABAP Cloud add-ons, reaching a larger customer base effectively.  Partners begin by developing their extensions within their own landscape and under their own namespace. After thorough testing, the solution is registered and assembled into an installable package. Once published by the partner, it becomes available to customers on the SAP Store. Customers can explore all their purchased and available products using the Discover Product app and can subsequently deploy these solutions in their three-system landscape.

Figure 8: Expand opportunities with scalable solutions.

 

 

This approach greatly complements the existing side-by-side extensibility concept to scale applications according to growing demand, ensuring they stay agile and responsive to rapid market changes. Partners can leverage these capabilities to innovate and differentiate themselves in the competitive market, aligning their offerings closely with customer needs and business requirements as an additional option.

For more information click here. 

 

Advancing Project Efficiency: Innovations in SAP Activate Release

In the ongoing evolution of SAP Activate, we are committed to enhancing our implementation and operational guidance, continuously leveraging insights from customer projects and, in this case, early adopters of Project Lines. Our latest release introduces refined guidance in project management, which enables teams to streamline their workflows and optimize resource allocation. We have also introduced improvements in receiving provisioned environments, making it easier and faster for customers to get started with their projects. Additionally, we have enhanced the identification of activities related to the GROW Adoption Plan, ensuring that users can seamlessly integrate these into their strategic roadmaps.

Another major advancement I’d like to mention is the integration of the “SAP Activate road map viewer” into the “SAP for Me” platform.

Figure 9: SAP Activate Road Map view.

 

 

This transition simplifies access for all users of the comprehensive methodology content of SAP Activate. Seamless learning and efficient project planning are important ingrediencies of a transformed demand with 5 generations of users.

 

Key Summary

In a nutshell, The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces a balanced mix of infrastructure and experience investments, enhanced AI-driven user experiences, embedded AI features, and new or enhanced ERP capabilities, rounded off by the innovative SAP Green Ledger for integrated carbon accounting within financial systems. Additionally, ABAP Cloud Add-Ons give partners an additional tool at hands to extend and differentiate solutions effectively.


Another release that paves the way for businesses to optimize efficiency, scalability, and sustainability in their transformed ERP operations. As all previous releases, it is our invitation to you to join us in the ERP revolution and start your transformative journey. Keep exploring us and keep challenging us to unlock your company's full potential in the evolving digital landscape. Stay tuned for further details in the individual blog posts diving deeper into selected lines of business and industries.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Feel free to follow me at BeSchulze and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates. 

Yours, Bert

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

  

🔎Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: 3-System Landscape Conversion in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Expert Talk
entry.content:
In a recent Expert Talk video, SAP experts and industry leaders gathered to discuss the strategic importance and technical nuances of transitioning from a 2-system to a 3-system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This blog post aims to distill the key insights from that discussion, providing SAP professionals with a deeper understanding of the 3SL conversion process, its benefits, and how to navigate the challenges effectively.

Watch the video below for a more interactive experience and to hear directly from the experts:

 

Introduction to 3SL Conversion

The session hosted by Yannick Peterschmitt kicked off with an introduction by Calin Cernea, product management expert from SAP's SaaS Foundation, who outlined the fundamental differences between the 2-system and 3-system landscapes. Traditionally, a 2-system landscape comprises quality and production environments. However, as businesses grow and their needs evolve, this setup may no longer suffice.

The 3-system landscape introduces a new test environment, which takes over the quality assurance tasks from the quality system, while the production environment remains unchanged. This addition is crucial for businesses looking to implement new projects without disruptions caused by system upgrades.

 Figure 1: 2-System Landscape to 3-System Landscape Conversion

Customer Insights: Making Science's Conversion Experience

Javier Mora, from Making Science, shared their company's journey of converting to a 3-system landscape. The primary motivation for this transition was to standardize business operations across various countries and minimize disruptions during system implementations. The 3-system landscape facilitated a smoother testing environment, allowing Making Science to configure their operations more effectively and with fewer interruptions.

Victor Alvarez, from the SAP partner Seidor, highlighted the collaborative effort between Making Science and Seidor in navigating the conversion process. From the early stages, Seidor was deeply involved, helping to elucidate the benefits of the new system landscape and managing the technical aspects of the migration. This partnership ensured that the conversion did not impact daily operations and integrated new functionalities seamlessly.

Preparation is key to a successful conversion. The discussion emphasized the importance of maintaining clear and consistent communication with SAP, leveraging detailed documentation, and holding regular alignment meetings. These practices help ensure that all parties are on the same page and that the conversion process proceeds with minimal disruptions.

Post-Conversion Activities and Best Practices

Post-conversion activities are critical to ensuring that the new system functions as intended. Calin from SAP explained that one of the primary goals of the 3SL/CBC conversion program is to minimize these activities, allowing customers to quickly resume normal operations. He recommended that customers engage with the available resources on the SAP community page, which includes how-to guides and videos that provide further clarity on post-conversion processes.

Find all relevant material in the SAP Three-System Landscape Community

The transition to a 3-system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers numerous benefits, including enhanced scalability, stability, and the ability to conduct thorough testing before production releases. As businesses continue to grow and face increasingly complex challenges, adopting a 3-system landscape can provide the flexibility and robustness needed to support continuous improvement and innovation.

Call to Action

For SAP professionals and customers interested in exploring the 3SL conversion further, the full Expert Talk video is an invaluable resource. Additionally, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 3-system landscape page on the SAP Community website offers extensive documentation and learning materials to support your conversion journey.

Engage with these resources to ensure a smooth and effective transition to the 3-system landscape, positioning your business for success in the dynamic digital landscape.

Take the chance to watch all our Expert Talk session available on YouTube.

 

 Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success Enablement & Adoption offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Welcome to 2025!

Our team has been hard at work, listening to your feedback and innovating to bring you a suite of new content. Whether you're navigating complex software enhancements, organizing release schedules, or simply ensuring your projects run smoother, these updates are crafted to keep you up to speed on the latest features. In this post, we’ll explore exciting new content provided by SAP experts, that is now at your fingertips and how it can significantly enrich your workflow.

Starting today, the content inside the Release Navigator will begin to be updated to 2502!

To reduce the impact on your user experience, starting after 2408, the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only includes new UX elements once a year, in August, when new release content is also updated.

Just a reminder, no tasks are required for the updates to the release navigator, as everything is automated. For more detailed information on product release dates, please refer to the Release Schedule card. Here you can find the UX innovation & content update plans for 2025.

 We value your input and encourage you to share any feedback or specific content requests for future updates to the Release Navigator. Please feel free to complete our feedback survey or contribute your suggestions in the Comments section below this post.

To explore more about how the Readiness@Scale team is dedicated to overseeing release readiness within the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio, visit our Release Ready page. Additionally, you can find all our team's blog posts by searching the tag  Readiness@Scale. Thank you for your continued engagement and support.

 

Best Run,

Readiness@Scale with SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: 基于按单生产和可配置产品的高级公司间销售
entry.content:
前提条件

使用7DM-基于按单生产和可配置产品的高级公司间销售，需要先激活以下业务场景：

1YT – Make-to-Order Production with Variant Configuration

BJE – Make-to-Order Production - Finished Goods Sales and Final Assembly

5D2 – Advanced Intercompany Sales

同时需要完成5D2和7DM相关Set-up instructions中所有的配置

客户需要通过创建事件申请Expert Configuration来激活高级公司间销售，可以先在测试系统申请并测试，如果发现细节部分无法满足客户需求也可以再次通过事件申请Expert Configuration来移除高级公司间销售的配置，从而可以继续使用经典公司间销售。

高级公司间销售与经典公司间销售无法同时并存。

流程介绍

从流程上对比经典公司间销售，高级公司间销售会产生公司间销售订单与公司间采购订单并且对应公司间销售发票和公司间供应商发票，有个细节是经典公司间销售流程中公司间销售发票是需要在创建完客户发票后才能生成的，而高级公司间销售并没有这个限制。同时高级公司间销售使用了在途功能。

系统实现

1.创建客户销售订单

业务场景：7DM不仅支持MTO并且支持可配置物料AVC：

2.通过APP：监控价值链，可以看到公司间销售订单和公司间采购订单由系统自动创建：

注意：公司间销售订单与公司间采购订单的价格分别需要通过condition record和purchase info record维护。目前系统无法产生关联创建。

思考：实际业务会有公司间销售订单与公司间采购订单两者价格不同的情况吗...？

3.进行MTO生产流程，请参考SI:1YT或者SI:BJE完成以下步骤

3.1 运行MRP （Material Requirements Planning for Sales Order）

3.2 创建生产订单（Create Production Order）

3.3 发布生产订单（Release Production Orders）

Area of Responsibility Production Supervisor: plant 1010; MTO VC -Valuated YB3

3.4 拣配组件（Pick Components）

3.5 确认组装活动（Confirm Assembly Activities）

对Operation 0010, 0031, 0032进行Post and Complete.

3.6 生产订单收货（Post good receipt for Production Order）

4.创建外向交货单5.拣配外向交货单和发货过账

发货过账前请检查内部和外部计划时间，计划时间设置在系统实际日期后，比如系统实际日期为2025.01.14，计划时间设定在2025.01.23

发货过账后查看产品库存状态，对应流程图中的步骤7：转移到工厂1010在途

6.确认实际转让日期

现在系统时间是2025.01.14，外向交货单内部转移计划时间是2025.01.23（在未来），此时我输入内部实际转移时间早于系统时间并且保存后：

系统自动生成以下物料凭证，对应流程图步骤8

查看此时库存状态，可以发现产品从工厂1010在途转移到工厂1710在途

根据实际业务情况，在外部转移确认时间字段输入时间后（早于系统时间），如下：

产品从工厂1710在途发货给了客户，对应流程图步骤9

注意：由于此步骤是在交货单PGI之后，所以在途库存没办法再进行拆分，比如需求：产品在工厂1010在途2件，请求先发1件到客户，这个是无法被满足的。

7.创建公司间销售发票IV2

系统会自动创建公司间供应商发票，APP：监控价值链同时会监控报错

8.创建客户销售发票F2

相关Blog

经典公司间销售:

基于MTO产品的公司间销售退货流程介绍 - SAP Community

基于MTO产品的公司间销售流程介绍 - SAP Community

感谢观看
